content.full_text:
John w. W1tt 
City Attorney 
C1ty Administration Building 
San Diego, CA, 
92101 
Dear Mr. Witt: 
June 11, 1979 
ReI 
Opinion Request 
No. 79-006 
II 
As you know, the Fair Political Practices Commission, 
at its meet.ing in Sacramento on June 6, 1979, 'concluded 
that San Diego Stadium passes provided by the City of 
San Diego to its employees and to the Stadium Authority 
need not be reported on Statements of Economic Interests 
regardless of whether the t.icket.s are used in accordance 
with Council Policy No. 700-22 authorizing the issuance 
of such passes. 
Among other things, this conclusion was 
based on your testL~ony that, despite the language of 
the Policy, the practice of the Council is to permit 
usage of the passes at any time, and that the passes are 
provided by the City to its own employees and to members 
of a joint powers agency in which it is a participant. 
The passes therefore fall within the exception provided 
in Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). 
If you have further questions, please contact me. 
MB:TP:plh 
Sincerely, 
Michael Bennett 
Executive Director

sections.conclusion: San Diego Stadium passes provided by the City of San Diego to its employees and to the Stadium Authority need not be reported on Statements of Economic Interests regardless of whether the tickets are used in accordance with Council Policy No. 700-22, as they fall within the exception provided in Government Code Section 82030(b)(2).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether San Diego Stadium passes provided by the City to its employees and the Stadium Authority must be reported on Statements of Economic Interests.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that San Diego Stadium passes need not be reported on Statements of Economic Interests because they are provided by the City to its own employees and members of a joint powers agency, falling within the Government Code Section 82030(b)(2) exception.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear legal opinion and conclusion in letter format. Opinion Request No. 79-006 referenced. Minor OCR artifacts noted (e.g., 'testL~ony' for 'testimony').

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether San Diego Stadium passes provided by the City to its employees and the Stadium Authority must be reported on Statements of Economic Interests.

CONCLUSION: San Diego Stadium passes provided by the City of San Diego to its employees and to the Stadium Authority need not be reported on Statements of Economic Interests regardless of whether the tickets are used in accordance with Council Policy No. 700-22, as they fall within the exception provided in Government Code Section 82030(b)(2).

Among other things, this conclusion was based on your testL~ony that, despite the language of the Policy, the practice of the Council is to permit usage of the passes at any time, and that the passes are provided by the City to its own employees and to members of a joint powers agency in which it is a participant. The passes therefore fall within the exception provided in Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). If you have further questions, please contact me. MB:TP:plh Sincerely, Michael Bennett Executive Director
embedding.summary: The FPPC advised the San Diego City Attorney that Stadium passes provided to City employees and the Stadium Authority are exempt from disclosure requirements under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2).
